author: Sofia Martínez
title: Build Your SMB Marketing Dashboard: Metrics That Actually Drive Revenue
description: Stop guessing on marketing ROI. Learn which 8 metrics matter for small business, how to connect them to revenue, and the free/cheap tools to track them.

sections.p: Last week, a client asked us: "Are my Google Ads working?" We pulled their data and realized they'd been measuring website clicks instead of qualified leads. They spent $8,000 a month without knowing if a single customer came from paid search. This isn't uncommon. We've found that 73% of SMBs don't have a connected analytics setup — they're running separate reports in Google Analytics, their CRM, and a spreadsheet they email themselves. A real dashboard changes that. When you can see how $1 in ad spend connects to $4 in revenue, every marketing decision gets easier.

sections.h2: The 8 Metrics That Matter (and Which Tools to Use)

sections.p: You don't need 47 metrics. You need 8. The best SMB dashboards track: website traffic by source, conversion rate by channel, cost per lead, cost per customer acquisition, customer lifetime value, email open and click rates, and social media click-through rate. For most service businesses and ecommerce, that's your north star.

list: Google Analytics 4 (free): website traffic, session count, conversion events, goal completion
Google Ads dashboard (free): cost per click, impressions, conversion rate, return on ad spend
Facebook Ads Manager (free): cost per lead, frequency, click-through rate, conversion value
Mailchimp or Klaviyo (free tier): email opens, clicks, unsubscribe rate, revenue per email
HubSpot (free CRM): contact source, pipeline value, deal close rate, sales cycle length
Looker Studio (free): combines GA4, Ads, CRM data into one dashboard

sections.h2: Connect Revenue to Marketing: The Attribution Layer

sections.p: Here's where most SMBs break down. You know you spent $2,000 on Google Ads last month. But did that $2,000 bring in customers worth $6,000 or $600? Without connecting your ads to actual revenue, you're flying blind. If you're a service business (plumber, dentist, landscaper), your CRM is the source of truth. Tag every customer with their traffic source: "Google Ads," "Organic Search," "Referral." Then calculate cost per customer and lifetime value per source.

sections.quote: Once we connected ad spend to actual customer revenue, we realized our Facebook Ads had 3.2x ROAS while Google Ads had 1.1x. We reallocated $3,000 monthly to Facebook and revenue went up 28% in 6 weeks.

sections.p: For ecommerce, use Google Analytics 4's conversion tracking combined with your Shopify or WooCommerce revenue data. Set up a simple UTM parameter system so every ad, email, and social post passes a source label through checkout. HubSpot's free CRM integrates with both platforms.

sections.h2: Build Your First Dashboard in 30 Minutes

sections.p: If you use Looker Studio (free), you can build a dashboard that pulls live data from Google Analytics 4, Google Ads, and your CRM — no coding required. Step 1: Connect your data sources. Step 2: Create a simple 2x3 grid with website traffic, conversion rate, cost per lead, customer acquisition cost, lifetime value, and revenue. Step 3: Automate a weekly email report. Most SMBs do this in under an hour the first time.

list: Use Looker Studio templates — don't start from scratch
Add a monthly comparison chart so you see growth week-over-week
Tag conversions properly: lead, qualified lead, customer, repeat customer
Review your dashboard every Monday; adjust ad spend by Wednesday
Set a 3-month goal for each metric (e.g., "increase conversion rate to 3.2%")

sections.p: If you're spending over $5,000/month on marketing across channels, a fractional analytics person (20 hours/month at $50-75/hour) pays for itself in optimizations within 30 days. They'll spot the Facebook audience that converts at 6.8% or the landing page with 58% bounce rate. For smaller budgets, a quarterly audit ($1,500–2,500) with someone who knows your CRM can unlock thousands in revenue.
